question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, which of the following is TRUE about fee collection from clients?

answer.text: Advance fees can be collected for up to 3 years if the client agrees
Fee limits and refund provisions apply only to individual and HUF clients who are not accredited investors
All clients including institutional investors are subject to the same fee caps
Investment advisers cannot charge any fees in advance under any circumstances

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The fee-related provisions (fee limit, modes of payment, refund, advance fee, breakage fees) apply only to individual and HUF clients provided these clients are not accredited investors. Advance fees, if agreed by the client, shall not exceed fees for one year. **The Trap:** Institutional clients and accredited investors are not subject to these specific fee restrictions. Also, advance fees ARE allowed but capped at one year's worth. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A and X-B both cover the fee structure for IAs. The concept of accredited investors is also tested in Series XIX (AIF) exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure fee agreements are documented and signed — verbal fee arrangements without written confirmation can lead to disputes and regulatory issues.
